name: Churrón de Borleña via the La Llana stream path
name_original: Churrón de Borleña por la senda fluvial del arroyo de La Llana
region_intro: Jogging routes around Corvera De Toranzo traverse a landscape rich in natural beauty, characterized by the serene riverbanks of the Pas River and the varied terrain of the Cildá and Espina del Gallego mountains. The region features ancient oak forests, lush alder and ash trees lining streams, and significant natural highlights like the Churrón de Borleña waterfall. Runners can explore paths alongside the river, through forests, and near the La Furta Wetland, offering diverse environments for outdoor activity. Best jogging routes around Corvera De Toranzo The most popular jogging route is Churrón de Borleña via the La Llana stream path, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail that takes about 30 minutes to complete. This route leads to a spectacular 20-meter natural waterfall through a picturesque forest. Another top favourite among local runners is El Caballar loop from Convento de San Francisco de El Soto, a difficult 17.2 miles (27.6 km) path. This challenging route offers extensive views across the mountainous terrain. Local runners also love the Running loop from Vargas, a 7.0 miles (11.2 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Jogging around Corvera De Toranzo is defined by verdant river valleys, ancient forests, and protected wetlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Corvera De Toranzo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 4 reviews. More than 400 runners have used komoot to explore Corvera De Toranzo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Corvera de Toranzo?
A: There are over 40 running routes around Corvera de Toranzo, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. These include moderate and more challenging trails through diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or families in Corvera de Toranzo?
A: Yes, Corvera de Toranzo offers several accessible options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4022279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pas Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, known for its flat and well-maintained surfaces, ideal for a relaxed jog. While rated moderate, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999045276/churron-de-borlena-via-the-la-llana-stream-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Churrón de Borleña via the La Llana stream path&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter 2.5-mile route that leads to a beautiful waterfall, making it a rewarding option for families.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see along the running trails?
A: The running routes in Corvera de Toranzo are rich in natural beauty. You can expect to jog alongside the serene &lt;strong&gt;Pas River&lt;/strong&gt;, through ancient oak forests, and past lush alder and ash trees. A significant highlight is the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4146541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Churrón de Borleña Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the area also features the &lt;strong&gt;La Furta Wetland&lt;/strong&gt;, a Special Protection Area for Birds.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more demanding experience, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38194836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Caballar loop from Convento de San Francisco de El Soto&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 17.2-mile path with significant elevation gain, providing extensive views across the mountainous terrain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38196746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Brazo Refuge loop from Los Corrales de Buelna&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Corvera de Toranzo?
A: Most outdoor trails in Corvera de Toranzo are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in nature protection areas or near livestock, and to carry waste bags to maintain the cleanliness of the trails for everyone.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Corvera de Toranzo are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point without retracing your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31970792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Vargas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24578111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Varga loop from San Felices de Buelna&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Corvera de Toranzo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 4 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from verdant river valleys to ancient forests and protected wetlands, and the well-maintained paths that offer a refreshing and engaging environment for outdoor activity.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest along the running routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4146541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Churrón de Borleña Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a popular natural landmark. You might also encounter historical sites or charming villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4022279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pas Greenway&lt;/a&gt; itself connects various points of interest, including the Alceda Spa and the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4620302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Viesgo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there running routes that offer scenic views of the mountains?
A: Yes, the region is characterized by the &lt;strong&gt;Cildá and Espina del Gallego mountains&lt;/strong&gt;, and many routes provide stunning vistas. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38194836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Caballar loop from Convento de San Francisco de El Soto&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive views across the mountainous terrain, allowing you to appreciate the dramatic landscapes of Cantabria.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Corvera de Toranzo?
A: Corvera de Toranzo's diverse landscapes make it suitable for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with lush greenery or autumnal colors. Summer can be warm, but shaded forest paths and riverside trails provide cooler options. Winter running is also possible, though some higher or less maintained trails might be more challenging.

Q: Are there any running routes that follow riverbanks?
A: Yes, the region is known for its picturesque &lt;strong&gt;riverbanks of the Pas River&lt;/strong&gt;. Many jogging paths run alongside the river, offering serene waters and surrounding greenery. The &lt;strong&gt;Toranzo Valley Nature Trail - Corvera Route&lt;/strong&gt;, for example, follows the banks of the River Pas, providing beautiful views through fields and rural landscapes.
tips:
This waterfall is located in Borleña (Corvera de Toranzo) and is formed by the Arroyo de la Llana, a tributary of the Pas River, which falls sliding from a large rock (sometimes with little water) but which reaches a beautiful pool at its feet that makes you want to put your feet in it.
The churrón is reached through a beautiful easy path, for all audiences and through a beautiful forest and that does not reach 2.5 km from the Bolera de Borleña.
